meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn a professional client workflow to solve project paralysis using Antigravity and Claude Code for beginners.
content.answer_block: The secret to project paralysis is shifting from 'what should I build' to 'what problem can I solve for a user.' By adopting a professional client workflow, you simulate real-world constraints. Use Antigravity to scaffold logic and Claude Code to refine your architecture, turning abstract ideas into production-ready portfolio pieces.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the graveyard of ambition. You sit down, fingers hovering over the keys, waiting for a spark of inspiration that never comes. You're trapped in 'tutorial hell,' consuming endless content but never shipping a single line of code that matters. This isn't a failure of skill; it's a failure of framework.

Professional developers don't wait for inspiration; they follow a workflow. By treating your first project like a client engagement, you replace the crushing weight of infinite possibilities with the clarity of specific requirements. It’s time to stop building apps for yourself and start building solutions for the world.

steps.title: Define the Client Persona
steps.description: Identify a specific niche (e.g., local bakery, freelance photographer) and list three pain points they face regarding organization or communication.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a technical product manager. Generate a list of 5 common administrative pain points for a freelance photographer and rank them by potential for AI-driven automation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Draft a project specification document based on the top pain point identified.'

steps.title: Architecture Blueprinting
steps.description: Map out the data flow. A successful student project needs a clear input-to-output path that demonstrates core logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-level technical architecture diagram in Mermaid syntax for a web application that automates client invoice generation using AI.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file architecture.md --add-content 'Include database schema and API endpoint definitions.'

steps.title: Rapid Scaffolding
steps.description: Use your tools to generate the boilerplate. Don't waste time on repetitive setup; focus on the unique business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a foundational project structure for a Python-based web app using FastAPI, including modular folders for services, routes, and models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize the project structure and create the virtual environment.'

steps.title: Implementing the MVP Core
steps.description: Build the single most important feature that solves the client's main problem. Ignore UI polish for now.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic function that processes raw text input into a structured JSON invoice format using pydantic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file services/invoice_generator.py --apply-logic 'Implement the text parsing function.'

steps.title: Refining and Documentation
steps.description: Add error handling and write a README that explains the 'why' behind your technical decisions—this is what recruiters look for.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md for this project that emphasizes the problem-solving approach, technical stack, and future scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a comprehensive README.md with installation and usage instructions.'

pro_tips: Always build for a persona, never for 'everyone.' Niche projects demonstrate deeper engineering empathy.
Focus on the 'Happy Path' first; edge cases are for version 2.0, not your first MVP.
Document your failures in your Git commits. It shows maturity and a debugging mindset to hiring managers.
Use Claude Code to perform 'rubber duck' debugging; explain your code to it and see if it finds logical gaps.
Limit your project scope to a 48-hour build window to force yourself to prioritize essential features over vanity UI.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is functional, deploy it to a free tier platform like Vercel or Railway and share the link on LinkedIn with a breakdown of the problem you solved.
content.conversion_block: Stop dreaming about the perfect project and start engineering the solution. Your portfolio deserves a real-world win that proves you can ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
